evals.prompt: I need a PowerPoint (PPTX) poster for my conference so my co-author can edit it afterward in PowerPoint. Can you build it?
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pptx-posters: confirms PPTX/PowerPoint was explicitly requested, builds the poster via the HTML/CSS template (poster_html_template.html), exports to PDF through the browser/Chrome-headless print path, and converts to PPTX (LibreOffice or python-pptx). Keeps content to 5-6 sections and 300-800 words.

assertions.value: Confirms the PPTX/PowerPoint requirement before proceeding and uses the HTML-to-PDF-to-PPTX workflow rather than producing a LaTeX poster.

evals.prompt: Make me an HTML-based research poster with a responsive layout that I can preview in the browser and print to PDF.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pptx-posters: copies the HTML/CSS poster template, fills in title/authors/sections with minimal text and prominent visuals, opens it in the browser for preview, and exports to PDF via the print dialog with background graphics enabled and margins removed.

assertions.value: Uses the web/HTML+CSS poster template and browser print-to-PDF path, enforcing the poster readability constraints (limited sections, large fonts, generous white space).

evals.prompt: LaTeX isn't installed on this machine — I still need a conference poster. Build me one I can produce without LaTeX and convert to PowerPoint.
evals.expected_output: Invokes alterlab-pptx-posters as the non-LaTeX fallback: builds the poster in HTML/CSS, exports to PDF, and converts to PPTX, since the user cannot use the default latex-posters skill.

assertions.value: Recognizes the no-LaTeX / PowerPoint constraint as the trigger condition for this skill and routes to the HTML-based workflow instead of latex-posters.

evals.prompt: I need a conference research poster — just a standard A0 academic poster, no particular format.
evals.expected_output: Defers to alterlab-latex-posters and does NOT invoke alterlab-pptx-posters: a plain 'research poster' with no PPTX/PowerPoint/HTML request defaults to LaTeX (better typographic control). pptx-posters only fires when PPTX/PowerPoint or HTML is explicitly requested or LaTeX is unavailable.

evals.prompt: Build me a PowerPoint slide deck for my 15-minute conference talk on machine learning.
evals.expected_output: Defers to alterlab-scientific-slides and does NOT invoke alterlab-pptx-posters: a PowerPoint slide deck for an oral talk is a presentation, not a poster. pptx-posters produces a single large-format poster, not a multi-slide deck.
